frameworks.name: uncertainty-guided exploration (directed/random/undirected)
frameworks.one_line: Hybrid exploration strategies sensitive to relative uncertainty, total uncertainty, and value difference.

frameworks.name: locus coeruleus-norepinephrine neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: Pupil-linked arousal indexes LC-NE activity driving random exploration.

frameworks.name: two-armed bandit / value-based choice modeling
frameworks.one_line: Choice model augmented with pupil-derived uncertainty estimates.

core_question: Does the pupil-linked arousal system encode the uncertainty signal that drives exploratory choice?
key_assumptions: Pupil size reflects locus coeruleus-norepinephrine activity
Exploration decomposes into directed, random, and undirected components
Total uncertainty and relative uncertainty are distinct decision signals
manipulations: Two-armed bandit task with varying option uncertainty
Trial-by-trial pupil dilation measurement during choice
key_findings: Participants used hybrid directed, random, and undirected exploration
Pupil size correlated positively with total uncertainty
Pupil-decoded uncertainty improved held-out choice prediction
interpretations: Pupil-encoded uncertainty causally informs which option to explore
LC-NE selectively drives uncertainty-guided random exploration
